scenes.title: Earthquakes: Causes, Measurement, and Hazards
scenes.narration: Earthquakes represent one of Earth's most dynamic forces, occurring when tectonic plates suddenly slip past each other, releasing vast amounts of stored energy.
scenes.visual: A 3D diagram showing two tectonic plates grinding and slipping against each other.
scenes.imagePrompt: No humans. Educational Illustration. Designed for College Students, 16:9 composition. Scene 1: A 3D diagram showing two tectonic plates grinding and slipping against each other.. Narration context: "Earthquakes represent one of Earth's most dynamic forces, occurring when tectonic plates suddenly slip past each other, releasing vast amounts of stored energy."

scenes.title: Earthquakes: Causes, Measurement, and Hazards
scenes.narration: This rapid release of stress along geological fault lines propagates outward through the Earth's crust in the form of seismic waves.
scenes.visual: An animation of seismic waves radiating outward from a cracked fault line.
scenes.imagePrompt: No humans. Educational Illustration. Designed for College Students, 16:9 composition. Scene 2: An animation of seismic waves radiating outward from a cracked fault line.. Narration context: "This rapid release of stress along geological fault lines propagates outward through the Earth's crust in the form of seismic waves."

scenes.title: Earthquakes: Causes, Measurement, and Hazards
scenes.narration: To understand these events, we must distinguish between key locations. The underground point where the actual rupture begins is called the hypocenter.
scenes.visual: A cross-section of Earth highlighting a glowing point deep underground labeled 'Hypocenter'.
scenes.imagePrompt: No humans. Educational Illustration. Designed for College Students, 16:9 composition. Scene 3: A cross-section of Earth highlighting a glowing point deep underground labeled 'Hypocenter'.. Narration context: "To understand these events, we must distinguish between key locations. The underground point where the actual rupture begins is called the hypocenter."

scenes.title: Earthquakes: Causes, Measurement, and Hazards
scenes.narration: Directly above the hypocenter, on the Earth's surface, lies the epicenter, which typically experiences the most intense and destructive ground shaking.
scenes.visual: A surface map showing a red dot labeled 'Epicenter' directly above the underground hypocenter.
scenes.imagePrompt: No humans. Educational Illustration. Designed for College Students, 16:9 composition. Scene 4: A surface map showing a red dot labeled 'Epicenter' directly above the underground hypocenter.. Narration context: "Directly above the hypocenter, on the Earth's surface, lies the epicenter, which typically experiences the most intense and destructive ground shaking."

scenes.title: Earthquakes: Causes, Measurement, and Hazards
scenes.narration: Seismologists track these movements using highly sensitive instruments called seismometers, which record the amplitude and frequency of ground vibrations.
scenes.visual: An animation of a seismometer needle drawing waves on a scrolling digital chart.
scenes.imagePrompt: No humans. Educational Illustration. Designed for College Students, 16:9 composition. Scene 5: An animation of a seismometer needle drawing waves on a scrolling digital chart.. Narration context: "Seismologists track these movements using highly sensitive instruments called seismometers, which record the amplitude and frequency of ground vibrations."

scenes.title: Earthquakes: Causes, Measurement, and Hazards
scenes.narration: While the historical Richter scale measured wave amplitude, modern science relies on the Moment Magnitude Scale to calculate the total energy released.
scenes.visual: A side-by-side comparison infographic of the Richter scale versus the Moment Magnitude Scale.
scenes.imagePrompt: No humans. Educational Illustration. Designed for College Students, 16:9 composition. Scene 6: A side-by-side comparison infographic of the Richter scale versus the Moment Magnitude Scale.. Narration context: "While the historical Richter scale measured wave amplitude, modern science relies on the Moment Magnitude Scale to calculate the total energy released."

scenes.title: Earthquakes: Causes, Measurement, and Hazards
scenes.narration: Seismic events rarely happen in isolation; they typically form sequences, beginning with smaller foreshocks and followed by disruptive aftershocks.
scenes.visual: A timeline graphic displaying a small foreshock, a major mainshock, and subsequent aftershocks.
scenes.imagePrompt: No humans. Educational Illustration. Designed for College Students, 16:9 composition. Scene 7: A timeline graphic displaying a small foreshock, a major mainshock, and subsequent aftershocks.. Narration context: "Seismic events rarely happen in isolation; they typically form sequences, beginning with smaller foreshocks and followed by disruptive aftershocks."

scenes.title: Earthquakes: Causes, Measurement, and Hazards
scenes.narration: Beyond immediate ground shaking, earthquakes trigger dangerous secondary hazards, including catastrophic landslides, fires, and devastating oceanic tsunamis.
scenes.visual: Icons depicting earthquake hazards: shaking buildings, a landslide, and a tsunami wave.
scenes.imagePrompt: No humans. Educational Illustration. Designed for College Students, 16:9 composition. Scene 8: Icons depicting earthquake hazards: shaking buildings, a landslide, and a tsunami wave.. Narration context: "Beyond immediate ground shaking, earthquakes trigger dangerous secondary hazards, including catastrophic landslides, fires, and devastating oceanic tsunamis."
